item.title: Update your ABC News homepage for more local stories
item.description: We've got an ABC News homepage for you. Adjust your settings for the right mix of local and national news.

item.title: Logies 2024 winners list: Larry Emdur wins gold, Rebecca Gibney honoured as Boy Swallows Universe cleans up
item.description: Here is the complete list of Logies winners, dominated by Boy Swallows Universe and Utopia.

item.title: Call for button battery redesign after new study reveals horrific injuries and fatalities
item.description: A new study reveals the horrific injuries suffered after swallowing or inserting button batteries but manufacturers aren't prioritising their redesign.

item.title: The US election is about style more than substance. It's why Kamala Harris needs to nail her DNC speech in about eight seconds
item.description: When the Democrat presidential candidate Kamala Harris delivers her speech to the Democratic National Convention today, style will be almost everything.

item.title: Reserve Bank restructure closer to reality after Treasurer Jim Chalmers offers olive branch to Angus Taylor
item.description: The Reserve Bank of Australia is a step closer to its biggest restructure in three decades after the treasurer offers an assurance to his Coalition counterpart.

item.title: Have Your Say: What was the issue, policy or event that decided your vote?
item.description: After a five-week campaign, the results are in with Labor returned to government. Is there a policy or issue that swayed your vote? Have Your Say.

item.title: Voters tell ABC's Your Say they want politicians with a vision, not bickering
item.description: Voters tell ABC's Your Say campaign they want courage and vision from politicians, not bickering.

item.title: Push for seniors' dental scheme grows ahead of federal election
item.description: Imagine living in aged care and not having your teeth or dentures properly cleaned and checked each day. That's a risk faced by elderly Australians, prompting calls for the next federal government to boost oral health training and services.

item.title: Australians tell ABC's Your Say they are struggling more than ever with housing costs
item.description: Voters tell ABC's Your Say campaign they are struggling more than ever with housing costs.

item.title: Australia's growing cohort of tenants fear they will rent forever
item.description: Home ownership rates in Australia have been falling for decades, and with rental vacancies at record lows and prices at historic highs, many fear their dream of owning a home may be out of reach. Some experts say rental caps could help.

item.title: Graduates in 'debt vortex' as HECS front and centre for first time in an election
item.description: New research shows a sharp rise in HECS debts, despite graduate earning power declining, as student loans become a potent election issue for the first time.

item.title: Housing advocates seek federal funding election promise for co-op model
item.description: Advocates admit co-operative housing models are not for everyone but say for the right residents, they offer more security and affordable pricing than traditional rentals.

item.title: Next-generation voters call for better engagement ahead of federal election
item.description: Young people in Albury say issues that directly affect them and their communities are not being addressed by major parties for the 2025 election campaign.

item.title: Melbourne seat of Calwell highlights Labor woes as Gaza pain galvanises Muslim voters
item.description: Dissatisfaction with the Albanese government's stance on Israel-Palestine among many Arab and Muslim voters threatens to unseat Labor MPs, in some places for the first time.

item.title: Cost of living, housing among key concerns in SA marginal seat of Boothby
item.description: At this year’s federal election, the South Australian seat of Boothby is one of the state’s two marginal seats. As part of the ABC’s “Your Say” campaign, we spoke to residents in the electorate about their biggest concerns.

item.title: Energy projects in South West WA threaten votes for major federal parties
item.description: Australia's two major parties have tied their federal election fates to controversial energy projects in WA's South West where the issues have polarised voters.

item.title: Tiwi Islanders eager to have their say as remote voting for the federal election begins
item.description: Voters in the small remote community of Pirlangimpi on the Tiwi Islands, north of Darwin, have become some of the first to cast their votes for the federal election.

item.title: Mobile coverage is an election issue in regional areas. What's been promised?
item.description: For many regional and remote Australians, reliable mobile coverage can be life-saving. They are desperate for both sides of politics to take meaningful action towards eliminating black spots.

item.title: Health, climate and community sport: What older voters in the Tasmanian seat of Bass are talking about this election
item.description: The federal seat of Bass is held by a margin of just 1.4 per cent. For these older voters who live in the electorate, health, climate and community sport are the issues that will decide their vote.

item.title: Go deeper with ABC's Long Read
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: The Price of Justice
item.description: Four Corners is the home of Australian investigative journalism.

item.title: Back to the Future the Musical's extraordinary visual effects mean you'll see the DeLorean fly
item.description: In the 1985 film Back to the Future, the DeLorean takes to the skies. So too in the musical production.

item.title: Some Australian states are set to get a free electricity period every day
item.description: The federal government says its new Solar Sharer scheme will also benefit Australians who aren't able to access it, but some experts are hesitant to agree.

item.title: In rare interview, World Safari documentary-maker Alby Mangels reveals why he 'disappeared'
item.description: In the 1980s, Alby Mangels was a box office star. The filmmaker wowed Australian audiences with his exotic adventures, but at the height of his fame he disappeared. In a rare interview he reveals what went wrong.

item.title: Adelaide Fringe and the high stakes for artists premiering original work
item.description: Forget getting any rest — actually, forget about a full night's sleep at all, because once you pass the point of no return in the production of a new show, the hits keep coming, the stakes get higher, and that deadline creeps closer and closer.

item.title: World's largest live export ship sails out of Darwin with 17,000 cattle bound for Indonesia
item.description: Anchored off Fremantle for weeks because the war in Iran had stopped the live sheep trade to the Middle East, the world's largest live export vessel has ventured north to Darwin.

item.title: WA rental price up 10pc since last year while availability at half national average
item.description: Western Australians are increasingly being driven towards financial distress and homelessness as the cost of renting continues to surge, according to new data from Anglicare WA.

item.title: Damian Buswell, Dennis Jung and Mark Mudie added to Fallen Firefighters’ Honour Roll
item.description: Hundreds of people have honoured the families and legacies of Western Australia's fallen firefighters at a memorial service in Kings Park on Sunday.

item.title: Why Chalmers will rein in the property free-for-all in the federal budget
item.description: After being rejected twice by an electorate that didn't want to give up lucrative tax arrangements on property, the Albanese government has sniffed the breeze and decided the time is ripe for change.

item.title: Rockhampton Regional Council sells six properties at unpaid rates auction
item.description: About 40 people attended the event, including buyer Nathan Casey, who snatched up a three-bedroom home for $200,000.

item.title: Kylie Minogue on career-spanning doco, Michael Hutchence, Nick Cave and the AFL
item.description: Australia's reigning Queen of Pop discusses her relationships with Nick Cave and the late Michael Hutchence, and being tapped to headline the AFL grand final's pre-game entertainment.

item.title: Air France and Airbus found guilty of involuntary manslaughter for Rio-Paris flight crash
item.description: France's national airline Air France and Airbus have both been found guilty of involuntary manslaughter over a 2009 crash that killed 228 people.

item.title: Is it worth getting a robotic lawnmower?
item.description: Experts say robot lawnmowers are becoming more accessible, but there are some key features to look out for.

item.title: Here's what experts want you to know about using AI to help with your finances
item.description: "Hallucinations", privacy and other factors to consider if you're turning to artificial intelligence for financial advice.

item.title: As Iran's internet comes online after months-long blackout, first posts send anti-regime videos viral
item.description: After being cut off from the world for more than 12 weeks, Iranians have come back online. Their initial reactions are joy, shock and confusion.

item.title: War hero and veteran MP Bill Grayden farewelled at state funeral in Perth
item.description: Hundreds of mourners farewell WA's longest-serving parliamentarian, war hero Bill Grayden, at his state funeral in Perth.

item.title: China warns Australia that 55 per cent beef tariff could apply within days
item.description: China is cracking down on beef imports and says it is ready to slap Australia with an additional 55 per cent tariff in the coming days.

item.title: Australian bull semen exports to China resume after pandemic pause
item.description: After a five year break, livestock farmers in China will use Australian genetics to produce premium beef for traditional dining and hot pot markets.

item.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: A peculiar peace prize, sky-high tickets and threatened boycotts
item.description: Struggled to keep up with the rollercoaster of controversies that plagued the build-up to this World Cup? We've got you covered.

item.title: Launceston City Council receives $100k in insurance after trophy, featuring 24-carat gold sheep, stolen
item.description: The trophy features a sculpture that includes a 24-carat gold merino sheep and a weaving loom made of silver and platinum, with its theft last year captured on CCTV.

item.title: Pig Feast explores Indigenous land rights in West Papua but key leader Mama Yasinta changed her tune
item.description: A key Indigenous leader in West Papua appeared in a documentary criticising an Indonesian government program, but then changed sides and disappeared. Her family suspects foul play.

item.title: Can you soar to the top of Guy Montgomery's Spelling Bee quiz — or will you be a sore loser?
item.description: From homophones to Aussie bird names, Guy Montgomery's Spelling Bee quiz is set to test and teach in equal measure.

item.title: Maroons rally around Tino Fa'asuamaleaui after father's death ahead of Origin II
item.description: Tino Fa'asuamaleaui indicates he plans to play in Origin II after the death of his father this week.

item.title: Hamilton claims famous Ferrari win, F1 leader suffers late car failure
item.description: Lewis Hamilton has wound back the clock and announced himself as a world championship contender again, claiming his maiden grand prix win as a Ferrari driver.

item.title: Eight crew presumed dead in B-52 bomber crash at US Air Force base in California
item.description: A US Air Force B-52 bomber, designed to carry nuclear and conventional weapons, crashes shortly after taking off at an air base.

item.title: 'Global' midwife shortage temporarily shuts Crystal Brook and Jamestown birthing services in SA's Mid North
item.description: Two more South Australian regional centres are now temporarily diverting expectant mothers as Kapunda Hospital's birthing service is closed indefinitely.

item.title: Ellyse Perry dominates with the ball as Australia trounces Bangladesh at women's T20 World Cup
item.description: Ellyse Perry dominates with the ball as Australia waltzes home to victory over Bangladesh in the women's T20 World Cup.

item.title: The White House is using anime as propaganda. Fans in Japan hate it
item.description: Both fans and the Japanese government push back after a series of Trump administration posts using images from popular anime and manga.

item.title: Magpies admit Bobby Hill will not make AFL return for some time
item.description: Bobby Hill returns to Collingwood training, but coach Craig McRae says it will be some time before the 2023 Norm Smith medallist is ready to play in the AFL again.

item.title: Sacred Victorian Indigenous site Lal Lal Falls runs dry as upstream 'greed' blamed
item.description: Indigenous and environmental groups are accusing a Chinese company of hoarding water on a Victorian property and causing a sacred waterfall to run dry.

item.title: Kyle Sandilands reveals new subscription-based show as his next move after $12m ARN Media settlement
item.description: Radio shock jock Kyle Sandilands reveals the details of his next move after accepting a "piddly little" settlement of $12 million in his contract dispute with ARN Media.

item.title: Move to prevent 'rivers of grog' as NT town camp alcohol bans to be extended
item.description: The NT government is extending alcohol bans in town camps beyond 2027, in a move welcomed by the health sector as a win for community safety.

item.title: Hanson's bulldozer moment could be a sign of new promiscuity in donor politics
item.description: One Nation's dramatic return to the national stage seems to have dashed any sense of restraint or shame about ties to wealthy donors.

item.title: Langbroek tight-lipped over Aboriginal leadership changes dubbed 'Project Invisibility'
item.description: Under the direction of Arts Minister John-Paul Langbroek, eight Indigenous directors have disappeared from the boards of south-east Queensland's cultural institutions.

item.title: Why fuel excise cut will continue to lesser extent until August
item.description: The fuel excise cut was due to stop at the end of the month but will continue to a lesser extent until August. Here's why.

item.title: Thunderbirds first team through to Super Netball Grand Final
item.description: The Adelaide Thunderbirds are through to their third Super Netball grand final in four years. Now they wait to see if they will face the Melbourne Vixens or West Coast Fever for a shot at the title.

item.title: Planning on writing off an impulse EOFY sales buy? You won't get all your cash back at tax time
item.description: Advertisers tout EOFY sales as a prime time for big buys, and hint at the promise of a generous tax refund, but that's not quite how it works.

item.title: AFL legend Tony Modra's family 'extremely grateful' for support after crash
item.description: The family of AFL icon Tony Modra has released a statement thanking the community for the "overwhelming show of love" they have received since he was seriously injured in a crash last week.

item.title: Ex-Wimbledon champion Markéta Vondroušová suspended for refusing doping test
item.description: Markéta Vondroušová, who lifted the Wimbledon trophy in 2023, is given a "significant ban" by the International Tennis Integrity Agency.

item.title: ACEREZ investigation finds failures during construction led to destruction of Aboriginal rock shelter
item.description: ACEREZ introduces strict cultural heritage protocols after a rock shelter was irreparably damaged.

item.title: Tax experts and self-managed super funds criticise Labor–Greens deal as 'policy on the run'
item.description: A ban on self-managed super funds borrowing money to buy housing has blindsided the sector, which says Labor should instead target "unscrupulous" operators spruiking property as an investment.

item.title: Chemical exposure limits put on hold despite Safe Work Australia advice
item.description: A majority of state and territory governments have put stricter workplace exposure limits on hold for nine hazardous chemicals.

item.title: Illegal e-bikes crushed after Tweed-Byron police crackdown
item.description: Police on the NSW Far North Coast have confiscated and crushed 35 e-bikes, and warn even more could soon be trashed as the crackdown continues.

item.title: Baby Kobi's death at Whispering Wall 'preventable' as coroner highlights 'errors'
item.description: A coroner finds the "upsetting" death of a nine-month-old baby in the Barossa Valley in a public murder-suicide by her "manipulative, self-absorbed and dangerous" father could have been prevented.

item.title: Housing Minister denies tax changes are major driver for market 'correction'
item.description: Clare O'Neil has acknowledged that the property market is undergoing a correction, but says the government's proposed tax changes are not the sole cause.

item.title: Third Australian H5 bird flu case confirmed in SA, another suspected WA case
item.description: A third confirmed case of the deadly H5 bird flu has been found in a migratory bird on Australia's southern coast.

item.title: Spotting the signs to stop the 'slippery slope' of gambling addiction
item.description: Experts say the gambling industry is increasingly targeting women, and there are potential risks with a novelty bet.

item.title: Hanson's 'monoculture' own goal should have been a free kick for Taylor
item.description: Pauline Hanson has chosen one of Australia's most popular national sporting brands and visible expressions of cultural diversity in her pitch for a "monoculture".

item.title: Royal Society of Tasmania turning colonial maritime art album into a digital flip book
item.description: The Royal Society of Tasmania has owned an album of Owen Stanley's maritime artworks since 1900, but its delicate nature meant it could not be put on display. Now, the society is converting the album into a digital flip book so everyone can see it.

item.title: Newcastle Knights player Asu Kepaoa banned for six weeks after using homophobic slur
item.description: Newcastle Knights player Asu Kepaoa is handed a six-week ban for using a homophobic slur during a NSW Cup match.

item.title: Stella Magnisalis fights to have ex-policeman husband kept behind bars
item.description: A domestic violence survivor speaks out about her fight to keep her former detective ex-husband behind bars as he attempts to be released on parole.

item.title: 'ISIS bride' on temporary exclusion order issued permit to return home
item.description: Australia's home affairs minister approves the return of a woman who has links to Islamic State and has been in a Syrian refugee camp, saying the government plans to have her under high-level surveillance.

item.title: Teals Allegra Spender and Zali Steggall form new political party Community Strong Australia
item.description: The teal independents have confirmed they are forming a new political party, Community Strong Australia, but are yet to convince crossbench colleagues to join what they call a "responsible alternative" to the major parties.

item.title: Decade-long fight for clean drinking water at remote Kimberley community
item.description: Residents of Pandanus Park continue their struggle to access safe drinking water amid a decade-long wait for a solution to dangerous nitrate in their tap supply.

item.title: Verified videos show impact of earthquakes in Venezuela
item.description: ABC NEWS Verify has authenticated videos showing the devastation after twin earthquakes struck Venezuela's coast.

item.title: Hanson's increased visibility comes at a price for One Nation
item.description: Parliament House has been Pauline's World lately but increased visibility comes with increased scrutiny and this week revealed that One Nation is not untouchable.

item.title: Labor eyes tougher laws to enforce social media ban for children
item.description: Australia will strengthen its social media ban for young people as a priority amid concerns the powers behind the world-first laws are not strong enough.

item.title: Qantas axes Alice Springs-Melbourne route, restricts flights between Darwin and Singapore to peak season
item.description: Qantas has axed its direct Alice Springs-Melbourne route after "many decades", and will also restrict flights between Darwin and Singapore to the peak tourist season.

item.title: ACT extends Indigenous Circle Sentencing in Supreme Court after successful pilot program
item.description: A pilot program allowing First Nations offenders to be sentenced by magistrates sitting alongside elders in the ACT Supreme Court secures funding for another three years.

item.title: High Court risk if Islamic State-linked woman barred from Australia, expert warns
item.description: Legal experts say the Commonwealth would have risked losing a High Court challenge to counterterrorism laws if it stopped the Australian woman from returning home.

item.title: Sexual misconduct findings not referred for Victorian Working with Children Check review
item.description: Victoria's former children's commissioner didn't refer sexual misconduct cases for a Working with Children Check review, despite saying she would never have done so in cases involving any sexual element "whatsoever".

item.title: Victoria's Alpine region police to carry long-arm rifles after Dezi Freeman officer murders
item.description: Chief Commissioner Mike Bush says Victoria Police officers in the state's alpine region will soon be permitted to carry long-arm rifles after the Dezi Freeman case.

item.title: Today show host Karl Stefanovic to leave Channel Nine, network confirms
item.description: Today show host Karl Stefanovic will leave Channel Nine after a controversial podcast interview with British far-right figure Tommy Robinson.

item.title: Influx of over-50s lifestyle communities on Capricorn Coast prompts mixed reaction
item.description: Four over-50s lifestyle developments have been approved for this small beach community, with two more being assessed. Some worry the area is not ready for the influx, others say it is the answer to the housing crisis.

item.title: Supergirl deserves better than a watered-down action movie
item.description: Australian Milly Alcock shines as Supergirl in the hero's first solo outing since 1984. If only the rest of the movie sparkled around her.

item.title: World Cup third place table: Which teams will make it to the knockouts?
item.description: The World Cup group stage is winding down, with third-placed teams anxiously watching other results to learn their fate. See the live table to see who is on track to progress.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Ecuador does the impossible, Sweden trains the house down
item.description: Ecuador's wildest dreams come true, the Swedes get a rude shock at training and a piece of World Cup history goes up for sale. Here are today's quick hits.

item.title: World Cup: Socceroos through to FIFA World Cup round of 32, but goals still hard to find
item.description: The Socceroos are into the knockout rounds of the World Cup, but they still have a big problem to solve.

item.title: How I save money by storing and cooking things differently
item.description: I've developed simple storage and cooking systems that keep food visible and fresh while ensuring I use things up. Here's how I make cooking for one feel more doable, affordable and lower-impact.

item.title: Small plane crashes into Beijing's tallest building, authorities seal off roads
item.description: Two bystanders have said an aircraft about the size of a car has crashed into Beijing's CITIC Tower.

item.title: US strikes Iran after Trump calls attack in Strait of Hormuz a 'foolish' ceasefire breach
item.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guards vows a "swift and decisive" response, according to Iranian state television, claiming they repelled a US attack on Sirik Island, on the shores of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Venue merch cuts are still putting artists at a loss, what needs to change?
item.description: Three years after a defining flashpoint in the dispute over merchandise cuts, venues and artists are still at an impasse.

item.title: Government to shake up to home loan subsidy scheme for Defence force members and veterans
item.description: Critics say the scheme, which offers subsidised home loans, lumps Defence members and veterans with uncompetitive rates.

item.title: How on earth has the global economy survived this ongoing war?
item.description: An unfortunate side effect of the incompetent and erratic negotiating tactics of the Trump administration is that some of the underlying stories in the global economy can get overlooked.

item.title: Water levels at Kati Thanda-Lake Eyre 'best' locals have seen
item.description: The myth of an inland sea lured 19th century European explorers into the heart of Australia. But where they found only desert, this year it's awash, with water levels at Kati Thanda-Lake Eyre now the best some locals have ever seen.

item.title: H5N1 bird flu a deadly threat to native duck species already menaced from all angles
item.description: Australia's native duck species were already threatened by urban sprawl, climate change, hunting and habitat degradation. Now H5N1 bird flu has been discovered on our shores.

item.title: Hikers lost in Kosciuszko National Park rescued by drones powered by AI
item.description: Rescuers were able to locate the hikers less than five hours after they were reported missing.

item.title: Queensland conservationists brace for potential bird flu battle
item.description: Home to the highest number of bird species in Australia, the sunshine state could have the most to lose from a bird flu outbreak.

item.title: NT Labor MLA Manuel Brown’s driving infringements further fuel party turmoil
item.description: Revelations about an NT shadow minister’s dicey driving history — and the Opposition Leader's failure to disclose it — come as another blow to NT Labor.

item.title: World Cup: Ousmane Dembélé suspends time as France's favouritism heightens
item.description: Ousmane Dembélé showed patience and a command of time and space, to help propel France to the top of its group, and remain on course for World Cup glory.

item.title: World Cup: Aziz Behich says he's 'gutted' for Matt Leckie, who has left the Socceroos camp with injury
item.description: Australia enters the World Cup knockout rounds with just 24 players in the squad after veteran Mathew Leckie and right-back Jacob Italiano departed the camp with injuries.

item.title: A warm and comforting gnocchi bake - ABC News
item.description: "This would be something I'd love to make on a Sunday and have it bubbling away while you're doing odd jobs around the house."

item.title: Witness captures aircraft fragments and building damage in Beijing
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Sacred Ashura tradition a show of 'resistance' in Beirut amidst Israel-Hezbollah conflict
item.description: Tens of thousands of Shiite Muslims gathered in Beirut for the largest Ashura procession in years, as Hezbollah used the holy day to project strength despite growing pressure to disarm after months of war with Israel.

item.title: Interactive exhibition encourages patrons to colour in art themselves
item.description: An art studio in Ballarat is hosting an interactive exhibition around a colouring book and visitors can contribute to the art.

item.title: Rich and famous families teach their kids Mandarin as China's influence grows
item.description: Rich and powerful families have a strong interest in one particular language, with experts saying it is no surprise given the country's rise.

item.title: Cabo Verde's run to World Cup knockout stage has them believing in impossible
item.description: Cabo Verde has secured the most unlikely spot in the World Cup knockouts, and the nation of 500,000 people is daring to dream.

item.title: Australian Simon Carman arrested after body of teenage girl found in suitcase in Thailand
item.description: A 45-year-old Australian man is in custody as investigations continue into the discovery of the body of a teenage girl found stuffed inside a suitcase.

item.title: Citizen scientists say hotline told them to collect dead bird against government advice
item.description: South Australian citizen scientists say they called the Emergency Animal Disease Hotline to report a dead sea bird and were given advice that contradicts official government messaging.

item.title: Iran says it hit US-linked targets as Bahrain reports drone attack
item.description: Also in the Strait of Hormuz an oil tanker has been damaged by an "unidentified projectile", while an Israeli drone has reportedly struck an area of southern Lebanon.

item.title: A brain disease is striking down Aussie rules players. At 23, Nick is the youngest
item.description: A talent and a team player, Nick Lowden excelled at Aussie rules. But the knocks, bumps, hits and collisions were adding up.

item.title: Vance vs Rubio: Which White House apprentice will win Trump's backing for 2028?
item.description: At times, Donald Trump appears to be harking back to his days hosting reality TV show The Apprentice as he leans into speculation about who he will anoint as his successor.

item.title: Karl Stefanovic's 'weird personality' might score him a Logie
item.description: Karl Stefanovic is no longer part of the Nine family but his audience may not be ready to abandon their prodigal son.

item.title: Best books out in June, including Whistler by Ann Patchett and Land by Maggie O'Farrell
item.description: June's best books include an Irish saga by Maggie O'Farrell, a gritty memoir by Ralph Jackman and a comic Italian romp by Andrew Sean Greer.

item.title: The truth about narcissists — and why most people get it wrong
item.description: As "narcissist" becomes the catch-all insult of the social media age, here is the crucial difference between everyday selfishness and a serious personality disorder.

item.title: Keith Urban on Tamworth, yacht rock and being an Aussie on the world stage
item.description: Keith Urban explains why he made a yacht rock album and how his Brisbane cover band days shaped Flow State.

item.title: Hanson's monoculture comments are from her three-step playbook and the Liberals played right into it
item.description: We've come a long way since it was Pauline Hanson who didn't know the meaning of big words.

item.title: How the Singha beer family is being torn apart by assault allegations
item.description: The billionaire family behind the Singha beer company is being torn apart by explosive allegations of assault and a century-old law that protects parents from neglectful children.

item.title: Electricity price spikes and batteries drain as wind drought tests green flag-bearer
item.description: South Australia is at the bleeding edge of the energy transition but record low winds have pushed its grid to the brink of its limits.

item.title: Venezuela earthquake death toll passes 1,400 as survival window narrows
item.description: The death toll from two earthquakes in Venezuela has climbed beyond 1,400, as searches for survivors intensify during the remaining hours of the window for survival.

item.title: Lindy Chamberlain murder trial artefacts and memorabilia paint picture of frenzy around 1982 case
item.description: Forty years ago, the chance discovery of a bloodied matinee jacket unravelled a murder conviction. Now, that evidence lives inside a national museum's collection that's too sensitive for public display.

item.title: Central Queensland family's 'heartbreaking' fight against motor neurone disease
item.description: A Queensland family shares its journey with motor neurone disease for the first time and gives advice on how to cope with a diagnosis.

item.title: The fastest human-powered Everest ascent from sea level
item.description: Avalanches, altitude sickness, orcs and Justin Bieber — a Brisbane mountaineer is believed to have set a new world record on Mount Everest for charity, despite every obstacle thrown his way.

item.title: Pauline Hanson says she has growing support in Canberra. But does it pass the pub test?
item.description: The conventional wisdom is that Canberra is a largely left-leaning city, but the national capital is now among places in One Nation's sights as support for the party surges across the country.

item.title: FIFA World Cup history beckons for Socceroos against 'fearless' Egypt in round of 32
item.description: Egypt is sweating on the fitness of star forward Mohamed Salah, but the Socceroos won't be complacent as they prepare for just the third FIFA World Cup knockout encounter in the team's history.

item.title: Young people from Australian and American cities find a home in outback Queensland
item.description: After escaping the big smoke, these five young people have swapped the metro rat race for a slower life in the outback – and say they wouldn’t change a thing.

item.title: US and Iran exchange strikes as Gulf tensions escalate despite ceasefire
item.description: The United States and Iran have exchanged strikes against targets in the Middle East, underscoring rising tensions that threaten the interim deal between the two nations to stop the war.

item.title: Key remote bird observatory shut for months before Australia's first bird flu detection
item.description: Volunteers and wildlife advocates push to reopen the Eyre Bird Observatory, arguing the station will be critical in monitoring the potential impact of avian flu.

item.title: NSW shark-spotting drone program expanded after Coogee Beach shark attack
item.description: Shark-spotting drones will provide year-round coverage at about 70 beaches along the NSW coast as part of an expanded surveillance program announced after a Sydney mother was attacked by a suspected white shark.

item.title: Griffith's Private Michael Paice and the war medal he never talked about
item.description: Private Michael Paice never talked about his distinguished medal from World War II, but his story of extraordinary bravery is now being told.

item.title: Long-awaited Perth-to-Bunbury train service reopens
item.description: The disruption-plagued Perth-to-Bunbury train service is back on track following years of breakdowns and delays, but locals remain frustrated over the rollout.

item.title: England through to knock out rounds, but flatter to deceive as pre-World Cup plans start to falter
item.description: England came into the World Cup with a side it thought was capable of beating the best teams in the world. If it can't beat the little guys, it may never get a chance to prove its theory right.

item.title: Thai teenager's family grieves as Australian charged with murder says death was 'out of my control'
item.description: The family of a 17-year-old girl in Thailand speak of their grief as an Australian man is charged with her murder.

item.title: Love story: Holding onto the glimmers of my dad, Blues legend Steve Mortimer
item.description: Erin Mortimer's dad, Bulldogs and Blues legend Steve Mortimer, was diagnosed with dementia at just 60 after years of concussions during his playing career. Erin is holding on to the special memories, and the glimmers of him that remain.

item.title: Bali's beauty sinks beneath an 'Island of Trash' as waste issues increase
item.description: Known to many as the Island of the Gods, residents fear Bali may soon become the "Island of Trash" due to a deluge of tourists, a lack of rubbish education and the partial closure of a much-needed landfill facility.

item.title: Treasurer Jim Chalmers promises to 'fix' property tax oversight to protect widows and divorcees
item.description: The Treasurer is promising to "fix" an unintended consequence of the government's overhaul of property tax breaks to protect widows and divorcees — but he has refused to say exactly how.

item.title: Hating UK PM Keir Starmer became a 'cultural phenomenon'. His friends are confused
item.description: It took only a month after coming to office for Keir Starmer's popularity to begin plummeting. Does it mean the UK has become ungovernable? Or is something else to blame?

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Salah's injury a Socceroos boost, Scotland's coach resigns and the tightest of offsides
item.description: The Socceroos' next World Cup opponents Egypt are sweating on the fitness of key striker Mohamed Salah, Scotland's coach Steve Clarke resigns after his team's exit and Davinson Sánchez was denied a late winner because of his toe. Here are today's quick hits.

item.title: Queensland police conduct counterterrorism training at shopping centres, stadiums
item.description: A scenario based on the deadly Bondi Junction shopping centre stabbings in 2024 has been used to test officers as part of specialist counterterrorism training.

item.title: New parents struggling with anxiety and depression urged to seek free counselling services
item.description: New parents often struggle in silence, but a growing number of perinatal clinics offering free mental health support are being rolled out across Australia.

item.title: Marija lost her husband five years ago but Meta won't memorialise his Facebook account
item.description: After Marija Milisavljević's husband died in 2021, she wanted to memorialise his Facebook account to keep a trove of photos and videos, but tech giant Meta has not made it easy.

item.title: Man charged after four-month-old baby's death in 2024
item.description: The 22-year-old Bundaberg man has been charged with domestic violence offences of murder and torture.

item.title: Young station ringers win Battle of the Barkly at the NT outback's Brunette Races
item.description: Based on the day in the life of an outback station ringer, the Battle of the Barkly obstacle course has been won by two young cattle industry workers.

item.title: Blues ring in the changes as To'o dumped for decider
item.description: New South Wales coach Laurie Daley has made five changes to the Blues side for Origin III with Brian To'o missing out for the first time in over five years.

item.title: World Cup: Who plays who in round of 32? Who do the Socceroos face? What time in Australia?
item.description: The World Cup has reached the round of 32. Here is the full fixture list for the remainder of the tournament, including the kick-off times in Australia.

item.title: Face paint, chants and flags — the many ways football fans celebrate the World Cup
item.description: From donning face paint to viral chants, each fandom has its peculiarities.

item.title: Jackson the star as Dockers make it 14 wins in a row
item.description: The Fremantle Dockers keep rolling along, winning their 14th straight game with a dominant victory over Gold Coast after the Kangaroos beat Essendon. Follow live.

item.title: Sydney and Melbourne record worst property auction clearance results in years
item.description: Sydney and Melbourne record their weakest auction results in years, with fewer than half of all homes going under the hammer finding a buyer.

item.title: Thousands gather for iconic Beer Can Regatta at Darwin's Mindil Beach
item.description: Competitors at the annual Beer Can Regatta race rafts engineered from drinking containers, with beer or soft-drink cans the popular choice this year.

item.title: World Cup: How the final qualification place was decided by 90 minutes of chaos between Algeria and Austria
item.description: Two stoppage-time goals, ghosts of a World Cup scandal from 42 years ago, and a desperately unlucky Iranian side are just some of the takeaways from Algeria and Austria's chaotic group stage finale.

item.title: Richard Scolyer's state memorial service to be held at Sydney Opera House on July 13
item.description: The former Australian of the Year and leading melanoma pathologist died on June 7 at the age of 59, three years after being diagnosed with an incurable brain cancer.

item.title: WA parents say they 'deserve' answers after baby dies from 'unknown cause'
item.description: The parents of Willow Horne are still looking for answers after the eight-month-old died in a regional WA hospital.

item.title: Kate's week four film, shot in Vanuatu.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Piastri has 'really good day', Russell survives late charge in Austria
item.description: Oscar Piastri has hailed his fourth place at Sunday's Austrian Grand Prix as a "really good day", while Mercedes George Russell held on to claim his first victory since the start of the season.

item.title: Australian run of Waitress closing early with Sydney shows cancelled
item.description: The Australian stage production of Waitress will no longer continue on its national tour, with planned shows in Sydney now cancelled.

item.title: Skydiving plane crashes in France, killing all 11 people on board
item.description: A small aircraft carrying skydivers has crashed in eastern France, killing all 11 people on board, local authorities say.

item.title: England captain Ben Stokes to retire from international cricket after third Test vs New Zealand
item.description: England captain Ben Stokes will retire from international cricket after the third Test against New Zealand, the team has said.

item.title: Live: Canada takes on South Africa in LA
item.description: World Cup co-hosts Canada takes on South Africa in Los Angeles as the round of 32 gets underway. Follow live.

item.title: This week in history quiz June 29: Test your smarts on Eminem, Jane Austen and competitive chess
item.description: This week was a big one in times gone by. From this decade to the 17th century, put your history knowledge to the test.

item.title: Do you qualify for three hours of free electricity? Here's how it works
item.description: Under the federal government's Solar Sharer scheme, some Australian households will be able to access three hours of free electricity a day. Here's how you can make the most of it.

item.title: Race Around The World recap: Catch the week four films
item.description: From survival vaults in Norway to a pianist in Pisa, catch up on the films from week four of Race Around The World.
